prompt: Rename the function reciprocalRankFusion to fuseRanks everywhere in this codebase (definition and all call sites). Keep everything compiling.
check: ! grep -rq 'reciprocalRankFusion' src && grep -rq 'fuseRanks' src/rrf.ts && npm run -s typecheck
why: Cross-file rename — Marksman's strongest case (one apply_edits vs N str_replace).

prompt: Move the file src/rrf.ts to src/fusion/rrf.ts and update every import that references it. Keep everything compiling.
check: test -f src/fusion/rrf.ts && ! test -f src/rrf.ts && npm run -s typecheck
why: File move + importer rewrite — exercises move_file / willRenameFiles.

prompt: In the BM25 class, change the default value of the k1 field from 1.5 to 1.2. Change nothing else.
why: Locate-then-edit a small change — measures the find cost (retrieval vs grep).

prompt: In the tokenize function (src/bm25.ts), raise the minimum token length so it keeps only tokens LONGER THAN 2 characters instead of longer than 1. There are two length checks inside the function body — `lower.length > 1` and `lp.length > 1` — change both to `> 2`. Change nothing else and keep it compiling.
check: grep -Eq 'lower\.length > 2' src/bm25.ts && grep -Eq 'lp\.length > 2' src/bm25.ts && ! grep -Eq 'lower\.length > 1' src/bm25.ts && ! grep -Eq 'lp\.length > 1' src/bm25.ts && grep -Eq 'parts\.length > 1' src/bm25.ts && npm run -s typecheck
why: Edit confined to ONE function body (two spots) — exercises surgical sub-node edits (set_body / replace_node target:body) vs re-emitting the whole function.

prompt: Add a required field `nameLower: string` to the SymbolEntry interface in src/types.ts — the lowercase form of the symbol's `name` — and set it at every place a SymbolEntry object is constructed (use name.toLowerCase()). Keep everything compiling.
check: grep -Eq 'nameLower: string' src/types.ts && grep -q nameLower src/indexer.ts && grep -q nameLower src/symbols.ts && npm run -s typecheck
why: Wide schema change: a REQUIRED field makes tsc fail until every construction site is updated — the first task that actually exercises the type-check GATE (nothing commits until the blast radius compiles) and the find-all-construction-sites step. `nameLower: string` (not `nameLower?:`) enforces required, so a passing tsc proves both sites were updated — ungameable.

prompt: Rename the SymbolEntry interface to CodeSymbol everywhere in the codebase — its definition in src/types.ts and every reference and import across all files. Keep everything compiling.
check: ! grep -rq 'SymbolEntry' src && grep -q 'interface CodeSymbol' src/types.ts && npm run -s typecheck
why: Type rename propagating across 5 files — scaled-up T1 (a function rename in ~3 files). Baseline must chase every reference/import and typecheck-iterate; a gated structural rename does the whole blast radius in one call. tsc + zero-SymbolEntry-remaining is ungameable.

prompt: This repo mixes Rust and TypeScript. Do both renames, everywhere (definition, call sites, imports): 1) rename the Rust function percentile to quantile; 2) rename the TypeScript function formatLatency to renderLatency. Keep the Rust crate passing `cargo check` and the TypeScript passing `npm run typecheck`.
check: ! grep -rEq 'percentile\(' src && grep -q 'pub fn quantile' src/stats.rs && cargo check -q && ! grep -rq 'formatLatency' web && grep -q 'renderLatency' web/format.ts && grep -q 'renderLatency' web/client.ts && npm run -s typecheck
why: Multilanguage: ONE session, edits gated by TWO compilers (rust-analyzer + tsc) via per-file provider dispatch, on a self-contained mixed fixture (`fixture` field — copied to a throwaway git repo per run). The Node oracle is TS-only, so `arms` restricts to baseline vs rust.

prompt: Two renames, everywhere in this repo (definitions and call sites): 1) in the Python job code, rename the function rollup_day to daily_rollup; 2) in the Go tool code, rename the function bucketLabel to tierLabel. Keep the Python byte-compilable.
check: ! grep -rEq 'rollup_day\(' jobs && grep -q 'def daily_rollup' jobs/rollup.py && grep -q 'daily_rollup(day)' jobs/rollup.py && python3 -m py_compile jobs/rollup.py && ! grep -rEq 'bucketLabel\(' tools && grep -q 'func tierLabel' tools/latency.go && grep -q 'tierLabel(u)' tools/latency.go
why: The GENERIC fallback provider: Python + Go in one session, neither with a native integration — structural tree-sitter edits, honestly UNGATED (the tool must say so). Measures whether the fallback still beats grep+Edit on find+edit efficiency. The Node oracle can't index either language, so arms are baseline vs rust.

prompt: Add a required field `burst: number` to the QuotaPolicy interface in src/core/policy.ts — the extra requests a caller may briefly make above the steady limit — and set it to 0 at every place a QuotaPolicy object is constructed. Keep everything compiling.
check: grep -Eq 'burst: number' src/core/policy.ts && grep -q burst src/middleware/throttle.ts && grep -q burst src/api/admin.ts && grep -q burst src/reporting/usage.ts && grep -q burst src/cli/config.ts && npm run -s typecheck
why: SCIP's claimed margin, isolated: every consumer imports QuotaPolicy through a BARREL (core/index.ts `export *`), and the gate expands ONE reverse-import hop. SCIP's semantic graph edges consumers directly to policy.ts (barrel flattened), so the reject enumerates every construction site; a syntactic import graph (CI_TS_MODE=treesitter-gated) sees only consumer->barrel->policy — the barrel itself never errors on a new required field, so the barrel consumers sit OUTSIDE the gate and a commit can claim clean while tsc fails. Run in `full` vs `treesitter-gated` to measure whether the SCIP read path earns its keep; tsc + per-file burst greps are ungameable.

prompt: This is a workspace monorepo (packages/core is imported by the other packages as `@acme/core`). Add a required field `timeoutMs: number` to the RetryPolicy interface in packages/core/src/policy.ts — the per-attempt timeout — and set it to 1000 at every place a RetryPolicy object is constructed, in every package. Keep `npm run typecheck` passing.
check: grep -Eq 'timeoutMs: number' packages/core/src/policy.ts && grep -q timeoutMs packages/gateway/src/proxy.ts && grep -q timeoutMs packages/reports/src/digest.ts && npm run -s typecheck
why: The MONOREPO seam: consumers import core via a BARE workspace specifier (`@acme/core`, root-tsconfig `paths` alias), which the syntactic resolver cannot follow at all — a bare specifier looks like a third-party package, so the fallback graph has ZERO cross-package edges and no transitive closure recovers them (edge-existence, not radius depth). SCIP resolves the alias through the TS compiler, so full mode's reject enumerates the construction sites in OTHER packages (e2e-verified: monorepo_bare_specifier test in lang-ts). Run `full` vs `CI_TS_MODE=treesitter-gated` — this is where the SCIP read path is load-bearing, not margin.
Cross-file rename on the shared corpus fixture — one task identity, the suite picks the language/repo.

ts.prompt: Rename the function blendScores to fuseScores everywhere in this codebase (definition and all call sites). Keep everything compiling.
ts.check: ! grep -rq 'blendScores' src && grep -q 'export function fuseScores' src/rank.ts && grep -q 'fuseScores' src/query.ts && npm run -s typecheck

rust.prompt: Rename the function blend_scores to fuse_scores everywhere in this crate (definition and all call sites). Keep `cargo check` passing.
rust.check: ! grep -rq 'blend_scores' src && grep -q 'pub fn fuse_scores' src/rank.rs && grep -q 'fuse_scores' src/query.rs && cargo check -q

java.prompt: Rename the method blendScores to fuseScores everywhere in this project (its definition in Rank.java and every call site). Keep the project compiling with javac.
java.check: ! grep -rq blendScores src && grep -q fuseScores src/main/java/com/corpus/Rank.java && grep -q fuseScores src/main/java/com/corpus/Query.java && javac -d "$(mktemp -d)" $(find src -name "*.java")

php.prompt: Rename the method blendScores to fuseScores everywhere in this project (its definition in Rank.php, including Rank::blendScores, and every call site). Keep PHPStan passing.
php.check: ! grep -rq blendScores src && grep -q 'function fuseScores' src/Rank.php && grep -q fuseScores src/Query.php && phpstan analyse src --level 5 --no-progress

swift.prompt: Rename the function blendScores to fuseScores everywhere in this package (its definition in Sources/Corpus/Rank.swift and every call site). Keep `swift build` passing.
swift.check: ! grep -rq blendScores Sources && grep -q 'func fuseScores' Sources/Corpus/Rank.swift && grep -q fuseScores Sources/Corpus/Query.swift && swift build

why: File/module move with import (or mod-decl/use-path) rewriting — the task that caught the staged-deletion false-clean.

ts.prompt: Move the tokenizer module: src/tokenize.ts should become src/text/tokenize.ts. Update every import so everything still compiles.
ts.check: test ! -f src/tokenize.ts && test -f src/text/tokenize.ts && grep -rq 'text/tokenize' src && npm run -s typecheck

rust.prompt: Move the tokenizer module: src/tokenize.rs should become src/text/tokenize.rs (a submodule `text::tokenize`). Update every mod declaration and use path so `cargo check` still passes.
rust.check: test ! -f src/tokenize.rs && test -f src/text/tokenize.rs && grep -rq 'text::tokenize' src && cargo check -q

java.prompt: Move Tokenize.java to a subpackage: src/main/java/com/corpus/Tokenize.java should become src/main/java/com/corpus/text/Tokenize.java (package com.corpus.text). Update its package declaration and every importer. Keep the project compiling with javac.
java.check: test ! -f src/main/java/com/corpus/Tokenize.java && test -f src/main/java/com/corpus/text/Tokenize.java && grep -rq 'com.corpus.text' src && javac -d "$(mktemp -d)" $(find src -name "*.java")

php.prompt: Move Tokenize.php to a sub-namespace: src/Tokenize.php should become src/Text/Tokenize.php (namespace Corpus\\Text). Update its namespace declaration and every referencing `use`. Keep PHPStan passing.
php.check: test ! -f src/Tokenize.php && test -f src/Text/Tokenize.php && grep -rq 'Corpus.Text' src && phpstan analyse src --level 5 --no-progress

swift.prompt: Move Sources/Corpus/Tokenize.swift into a subdirectory: it should become Sources/Corpus/Text/Tokenize.swift. In a SwiftPM target, files are members by directory, so no import changes are needed — just keep `swift build` passing.
swift.check: test ! -f Sources/Corpus/Tokenize.swift && test -f Sources/Corpus/Text/Tokenize.swift && swift build

why: Locate by CONCEPT, not filename — retrieval quality decides how fast the site is found.

ts.prompt: The rank-fusion tail dampening constant in this codebase is too aggressive. Find it and change its value to 90. Keep everything compiling.

rust.prompt: The rank-fusion tail dampening constant in this crate is too aggressive. Find it and change its value to 90.0. Keep `cargo check` passing.

java.prompt: The rank-fusion tail dampening constant RRF_K in Rank.java is too aggressive. Find it and change its value to 90.0f. Keep the project compiling with javac.

php.prompt: The rank-fusion tail dampening constant RRF_K in Rank.php is too aggressive. Find it and change its value to 90.0. Keep PHPStan passing.

swift.prompt: The rank-fusion tail dampening constant RRF_K in Sources/Corpus/Rank.swift is too aggressive. Find it and change its value to 90.0. Keep `swift build` passing.

why: One statement inserted at a described position inside an existing function.

ts.prompt: In the query search function, add an early return: when `top === 0` (or top <= 0), return an empty array immediately, before any lookups run. Keep everything compiling.

rust.prompt: In the query search function, add an early return: when `top == 0`, return an empty Vec immediately, before any lookups run. Keep `cargo check` passing.

java.prompt: In Query.search, add an early return: when `top == 0`, return an empty list immediately, before any lookups run. Keep the project compiling with javac.

php.prompt: In Query::search, add an early return: when `$top === 0`, return an empty array immediately, before any lookups run. Keep PHPStan passing.

swift.prompt: In the search function (Sources/Corpus/Query.swift), add an early return: when `top == 0`, return an empty array immediately, before any lookups run. Keep `swift build` passing.

why: Append a NEW top-level function to an existing module — the op insert_before can't express (no later anchor exists).

ts.prompt: Add a new exported function isStopword(token: string): boolean to the tokenize module: it returns true when the token is one of the common English stopwords "the", "a", "an", "and", "or", "of". Don't wire it into tokenize() yet — just add the function. Keep everything compiling.
ts.check: grep -q 'export function isStopword' src/tokenize.ts && npm run -s typecheck

rust.prompt: Add a new public function is_stopword(token: &str) -> bool to the tokenize module: it returns true when the token is one of the common English stopwords "the", "a", "an", "and", "or", "of". Don't wire it into tokenize() yet — just add the function. Keep `cargo check` passing.
rust.check: grep -q 'pub fn is_stopword' src/tokenize.rs && cargo check -q

java.prompt: Add a new public static method `boolean isStopword(String token)` to Tokenize.java: it returns true when the token is one of the common English stopwords (the, a, an, and, or, of, to, in, is). Keep the project compiling with javac.

php.prompt: Add a new public static method `isStopword(string $token): bool` to Tokenize.php: it returns true when the token is one of the common English stopwords (the, a, an, and, or, of, to, in, is). Keep PHPStan passing.
php.check: grep -q 'function isStopword' src/Tokenize.php && phpstan analyse src --level 5 --no-progress

swift.prompt: Add a new public function `isStopword(_ token: String) -> Bool` to Sources/Corpus/Tokenize.swift: it returns true when the token is one of the common English stopwords (the, a, an, and, or, of, to, in, is). Keep `swift build` passing.

why: The gate exercise: a REQUIRED field on a type constructed in two files — the compiler fails until every site is updated, ungameable in both languages.

ts.prompt: Add a required field `nameLower: string` to the DocEntry interface — the lowercase form of the entry's `name` — and set it at every place a DocEntry object is constructed (use name.toLowerCase() or the equivalent). Keep everything compiling.
ts.check: grep -q 'nameLower: string' src/types.ts && grep -q 'nameLower' src/store.ts && grep -q 'nameLower' src/query.ts && npm run -s typecheck

rust.prompt: Add a required field `pub name_lower: String` to the DocEntry struct — the lowercase form of the entry's `name` — and set it at every place a DocEntry is constructed (use name.to_lowercase() or the equivalent). Keep `cargo check` passing.
rust.check: grep -q 'pub name_lower: String' src/types.rs && grep -q 'name_lower' src/store.rs && grep -q 'name_lower' src/query.rs && cargo check -q

java.prompt: Add a required field `String nameLower` to DocEntry (DocEntry.java) — the lowercased form of `name`. Add it as a required constructor parameter (do NOT derive it inside the constructor body) and pass `name.toLowerCase()` explicitly at every construction site (Store.java and Query.java). Keep the project compiling with javac.
java.check: grep -q nameLower src/main/java/com/corpus/DocEntry.java && grep -q toLowerCase src/main/java/com/corpus/Store.java && grep -q toLowerCase src/main/java/com/corpus/Query.java && javac -d "$(mktemp -d)" $(find src -name "*.java")

php.prompt: Add a required promoted constructor property `string $nameLower` to DocEntry (DocEntry.php) — the lowercased form of `name`. Make it a required constructor parameter (do NOT derive it inside the constructor body) and pass `strtolower(...)` explicitly at every construction site (Store.php and Query.php). Keep PHPStan passing.
php.check: grep -q nameLower src/DocEntry.php && grep -q strtolower src/Store.php && grep -q strtolower src/Query.php && phpstan analyse src --level 5 --no-progress

swift.prompt: Add a required stored property `nameLower: String` to the DocEntry struct (Sources/Corpus/Types.swift) — the lowercased form of `name`. Add it as a required initializer parameter (do NOT derive it inside the initializer) and pass `name.lowercased()` explicitly at every construction site (Store.swift and Query.swift). Keep `swift build` passing.
swift.check: grep -q nameLower Sources/Corpus/Types.swift && grep -q nameLower Sources/Corpus/Store.swift && grep -q nameLower Sources/Corpus/Query.swift && swift build

why: Type rename across four files (impls / type-only imports included).

ts.prompt: Rename the interface DocEntry to IndexEntry everywhere in this codebase (definition and every use, including type-only imports). Keep everything compiling.
ts.check: ! grep -rq 'DocEntry' src && grep -q 'export interface IndexEntry' src/types.ts && npm run -s typecheck

rust.prompt: Rename the struct DocEntry to IndexEntry everywhere in this crate (definition, impls, and every use). Keep `cargo check` passing.
rust.check: ! grep -rq 'DocEntry' src && grep -q 'pub struct IndexEntry' src/types.rs && cargo check -q

java.prompt: Rename the class DocEntry to IndexEntry everywhere in this project. Java requires the file name to match the public class, so rename DocEntry.java to IndexEntry.java too, and update every reference. Keep the project compiling with javac.
java.check: ! grep -rq DocEntry src && test -f src/main/java/com/corpus/IndexEntry.java && grep -q 'class IndexEntry' src/main/java/com/corpus/IndexEntry.java && javac -d "$(mktemp -d)" $(find src -name "*.java")

php.prompt: Rename the class DocEntry to IndexEntry everywhere in this project. PSR-4 requires the file name to match the class, so rename DocEntry.php to IndexEntry.php too, and update every reference. Keep PHPStan passing.

swift.prompt: Rename the struct DocEntry to IndexEntry everywhere in this package (its definition in Types.swift and every use). Keep `swift build` passing.
